entry.title: Re: Cannot install Booster - SAP Integration Suite application is not subscribed.

Hi Nuri,

You need to activate capabilities first also you need to check in entitlement that "Process Integration Runtime" is already entitled or not. otherwise, just edit your entitlement and add "process integration runtime" as service (keep it mind you need api and integration both) if you have activated API Management and Cloud Integrations. after that you will be able to run the booster successfully. 
Hope it will resolve your issue. 

Regards,

Hira

entry.title: Re: Accessing HANA/ECC without BAPIs and Gateway?
entry.content:
> I'm still unsure of the best method to access my ECC database information.

I think you are trying to do the wrong thing... An ECC is NOT a database, it provides "business functionality", which includes much more than simple "data storage and search/query functionality": from sanity checks on the "plausibility" or logical integrity of business data to authorization checks to make sure unqualified persons cannot see data that is not meant for them... So you should not regard your ECC system as simply a "database", (in which case you could simply get an ODBC driver and access the database below the ECC system, but this is not to be recommended...)

Nevertheless, the answer to your question is "yes".
(But I guess, this is not the answer you are looking for... 🙂 The problem is, that your question is too general. A typical ECC system has thousands (maybe millions?) of interfaces, some RFC-based, some SOAP/WebService-based, some REST-Service based, others IDoc/EDI-flatfile based, etc... No one from the low level technical groups like "ABAP", "Cloud Connector" will be able to give you a meaningful answer here. First you need to find out, which data/information you want -- e.g. product descriptions from the Materials Management application or invoice details from the purchase orders of your customers -- and then label your question with the corresponding application (like SAP MM) to get the attention of the experts from that area. They will be able to tell you, which interfaces this application provides. Some applications may only have exposed ALE or RFC interfaces, others only SOAP or REST interfaces, and others both types, in order to be callable from "both worlds" (the RFC world and the HTTP world).)

entry.title: Shape the Future: Join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 3, 2024!
entry.content:
We're excited to announce that the third cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2024 has begun, featuring 33 new projects designed to enhance innovation and collaboration for our valued SAP customers and partners.

In today's rapidly evolving business environment, staying ahead is crucial. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative offers you a unique chance to gain early insights into SAP's product developments. By participating, you'll collaborate directly with our developers and product managers, influencing the future direction of our products.

Act quickly! Registration for the projects is open until November 15, 2024. Don’t miss this chance to make your voice heard and shape the next generation of SAP solutions.

To assist you in exploring the projects, we’ve created a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the opportunities available:

SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Composable Enterprise: Reimagining Enterprise Software for Unprecedented Business Agility 
  • Help Where and When You Need It: User Assistance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Improve your Post Upgrade Tests(PUT) Experience - An Integrated and Smart approach to PUT
  • Insightful Analytics for Actual Costing
  • Joule applications to SAP S/4HANA Utilities
  • Monitoring End to End Master Data Replications in SAP Cloud ALM
  • New Cost Settlement for real estate scenarios (Service Charge Settlement / CAM)
  • Purpose as base for processing of S/4HANA Business Partner personal data – concept and migration”
  • Research Project: Production Scheduling using AI
  • Research: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit - AI: Field Mapping Proposal
  • Using AI to Extract Data from the Delivery Note to Update the Inbound Delivery

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI Agent Builder - A Low-Code Innovation with SAP Build
  • AI-Powered Autonomous Sandbox
  • Cloud Security: Improving the Encryption Key Management Experience
  • Discover SAP Build Process Automation Triggers: Empowering Experts to Automate and Extend Use Cases
  • GenAI Content Provenance and Authenticity via Watermarking
  • Investigation into a Customer Security Dashboard
  • Knowledge Graph RAG on unstructured Data

SAP Concur

  • Concur Expense - India GST handling
  • Concur Expense – Integrating Sales Opportunity Data with Concur Travel and Expense Data
  • Concur Expense - Preventing overspend through cost control, spend planning, and regulatory
  • Concur Expense - SAP Integration with Concur Solutions: Employee- and Benefit- focused Budget Management
  • Concur Expense – The Future of Expense Administration
  • Concur Travel - Trip Management / New Trip List
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Manage and track carbon consumption in T&E spend

Customer Experience

  • Redesigned SAP Sales Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Outlook

Data & Analytics

  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

SAP Globalization

  • AI-supported Extension of Electronic Document Processing of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
  • Localization as a Self-Service for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with Business AI
  • Sales and Use Tax Reporting in United States - User Research

Lifecycle Management

  • Business Service Management in SAP Cloud ALM operations
  • Clean Core / RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard in SAP Cloud ALM

Others

  • Early Talent Management with SAP SuccessFactors

We believe that collaboration is essential for driving innovation. By partnering with our customers and partners, we can collectively shape the future of SAP’s products and revolutionize business operations.

So, mark your calendars, rally your team, and let’s embark on this journey together. Together, we can foster progress, and transform industries.

Don’t wait—register now at influence.sap.com and join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 3 in 2024!

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Re: How to control externally the iflow in integration suite?
entry.content: There are several different options on how you can accomplish this. Below are some examples - 1. You could you externalization parameters of content modifier flow step. 2. You Could design iFlow to use Sender adapters like HTTPS, SOAP etc to be invoked as an endpoint from external applications 3. You could create an artifact of type OData Artifact 4. using APIM, You could create a proxy wrapper on top of the conventional artifact and invoke it in a form or API from external applications. 5. There could several more other combinations possible. So, what is your business requirement. What are you doing in the in the ABAP program with PO comm channel. Is your requirement to start & stop channel from external applications. If yes, you can invoke oDATA API to undeploy/re-deploy iFlow based on router condition flow step or design an API & wrap it in APIM. So, options are unlimited. Hopefully if you provide additional details on your business requirement, a better narrowed down option may be suggested.

entry.title: Re: How to control externally the iflow in integration su...
entry.content:
There are several different options on how you can accomplish this. Below are some examples -

1. You could you externalization parameters of content modifier flow step.

2. You Could design iFlow to use Sender adapters like HTTPS, SOAP etc to be invoked as an endpoint from external applications

3. You could create an artifact of type OData Artifact

4. using APIM, You could create a proxy wrapper on top of the conventional artifact and invoke it in a form or API from external applications.

5. There could several more other combinations possible.

So, what is your business requirement. What are you doing in the in the ABAP program with PO comm channel. Is your requirement to start & stop channel from external applications. If yes, you can invoke oDATA API to undeploy/re-deploy iFlow based on router condition flow step or design an API & wrap it in APIM.

So, options are unlimited. Hopefully if you provide additional details on your business requirement, a better narrowed down option may be suggested.

entry.title: #05 - Understanding Project Methodology: The SAP Activate Methodology
entry.content:
The Importance of project management:

Effective project management is vital for delivering projects on time, within scope, and within budget. It provides structure and direction, helping teams navigate complex tasks and ensuring that everyone is aligned with the project's goals. Without a clear project management framework, projects can easily fall victim to scope creep, missed deadlines, and cost overruns.

Some common project management methodologies include:

  • Waterfall: A linear and sequential approach where each phase depends on the deliverables of the previous one.
  • Agile: An iterative approach that allows for flexibility and continuous improvement, often used in software development.
  • Scrum: A subset of Agile, focused on delivering smaller pieces of a project in "sprints" or time-boxed intervals.
  • Lean: A methodology that focuses on delivering maximum value to the customer by minimizing waste.

Each of these methodologies has its strengths, and the choice of methodology often depends on the nature of the project and the organization’s needs.

Introducing the SAP Activate Methodology:

At SAP, we’ve introduced a specialized project management methodology known as the SAP Activate Methodology. This methodology is designed specifically for SAP implementations and is tailored to meet the unique needs of businesses undergoing digital transformation.

What is the SAP Activate Methodology?

The SAP Activate Methodology is a simple, modular, and agile methodology used for implementing SAP solutions. It aims to accelerate implementations and drive continuous innovation by providing a standardized but flexible approach using best practices, agile principles, and a phased approach.

Key characteristics of the SAP Activate Methodology are:

  • Business Ownership: The business owns the solution, ensuring that the project aligns closely with business goals and delivers real value.
  • Lead with ‘Standard’ Best Practices: SAP Activate promotes using standard, pre-configured solutions that are based on SAP's best practices, minimizing the need for customization.
  • Agile, Modular, Scalable: The methodology supports an agile, scalable approach that can be adapted to different project sizes and complexities.
  • Rapid, Repeatable Delivery Steps: SAP Activate focuses on delivering value quickly and efficiently through repeatable steps.
  • Accelerators: Tools, templates, and content are provided to speed up the implementation process.
  • Quicker ROI: By using a fit-to-standard approach and best practices, organizations can achieve quicker return on investment.
  • SAP Solutions: designed to work with all SAP solutions, not just S/4HANA.
  • Six core project phases: Discover, Prepare, Explore, Realize, Deploy and Run.

Phases and main activities of the SAP Activate Methodology:

Access the SAP Activate Methodology tasks and accelerators via the SAP Roadmap Viewer:

The SAP Roadmap Viewer is a tool that provides access to SAP Activate Methodology implementation roadmaps. It offers a comprehensive view of project teams' activities, deliverables, and tasks associated with SAP implementations in in a structured way to support project planning and execution. It includes accelerator assets in document and hyperlink format to support implementation projects. The roadmaps are organized by logical categories or by SAP solution.

Recommendations for Success with the SAP Activate Methodology:

To fully leverage the SAP Activate Methodology, consider the following recommendations:

  • Learn about the SAP Activate Methodology: start with the Learning Journey Discovering SAP Activate Implementation Tools and Methodology to get a comprehensive understanding of how to manage your SAP projects using this methodology.

  • Join the SAP Activate Community: Engage with SAP Activate Methodology experts by joining the SAP Activate Community. This platform allows you to ask questions, share insights, and stay up-to-date with the latest news and developments in SAP Activate.

  • Explore the SAP Roadmap Viewer: The SAP Roadmap Viewer provides detailed guidance for your digital transformation journey. You can view and download SAP Activate Methodology assets and accelerators and even provide feedback to SAP.

  • Adapt to your project situation: While the SAP Activate Methodology provides a robust framework, it’s important to adapt the methodology to your specific project situation. Trust the process, but be flexible enough to make adjustments as needed to ensure the best outcomes.

Conclusion:

Understanding and implementing the right project methodology is crucial to the success of your SAP project. The SAP Activate Methodology offers a powerful, business-driven approach that aligns closely with best practices and the needs of modern businesses. By following the SAP Activate Methodology, engaging with the community, and utilizing available resources, you can ensure that your project is not only successful but also delivers rapid and sustainable value to your organization.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

  • Inês Moura supports with the design and orchestration of the customer onboarding journey for SAP RISE customers, ensuring a seamless experience from signature to successful go-live. As an Onboarding Advisor, Inês provides expert guidance and enablement, facilitating a smooth transition from the sales cycle to project start. With a focus on targeted project guidance and risk mitigation, Inês is dedicated to supporting SAP customers throughout the entire onboarding journey.

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @InesMoura or @Linh-Hoang .

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: GetVariableValues not working at first display on view mode
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We have a bex query with customer exit variable.
We create a new optimized story on live connection to this bex query.

We have this small script on the onActive Function of the Chart 1 :

var VariableQueryCurrentWeek = Chart_1.getDataSource().getVariableValues("XXXX")[0];
CurrentYear = cast(Type.SingleVariableValue, VariableQueryCurrentWeek).value;
console.log(CurrentYear);

(Note that is the same for : Application.getFileDataSource("CVAZVJGAC").getVariableValues("XXXX"); )

When we first launch the story in view mode we have the following error message on the console : " Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'value') "
Which is normal as it haven't found any value for this variable even if the bex query is well filtered according to the customer exit variable.
But then when i change the page on the story and comeback to the previous one, now it is working fine.
I don't really understand what hapenned

Do you have any idea how to fix this issue and have the variable the first time we load the page in view mode ?
Thank you in advance

Best Regards

entry.title: Populate UDFs from BOM to Production Model
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Please advise how to technically implement the following requirement in SAP Business One:

We have an UDF in Production BoM title, and the same UDF in Production Order title. The requirement is that whenever a Production Order is created for the respective product/BoM, the UDF from the BoM must be copied to the Production Order.

I know how to accomplish this with UI API when a user would select the product in a new Production Order. But this is only a partial solution, since a Production Order can be created in other ways without user opening a Production Order form, e.g. by using the MRP run.

I could use the SP_PostTransactionNotice and copy the UDF from there at database level, but since the UDFs are on SAP standard tables, I understand this is a no-go because of support policy.

Another approach would be using SP_PostTransactionNotice that writes the queue of to-be-updated production orders to a specific UDT, and then have an external application to monitor that UDT and update Production Orders using Service Layer. But isn't this a bit of an overkill for such a seemingly simple requirement?

entry.title: Re: API Freight Order or CDS (with Loading Location and U...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We have the API_FREIGHTORDER OData API, which can be used to retrieve Stop data, including the Location and the Stop Role.

Regarding CDS views, we have the I_FreightOrder*TP BO Interface Views, which are released for developer extensibility. This also includes the I_FreightOrderStopTP view which can be used to retrieve the Location and the Stop Role.

With the next Cloud Release, we will also C1 release the I_TransportationOrderStop_2 CDS basic view, which could be helpful for you in the future too.

 

I hope this helps!

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: crystal viewer parameter loses value on navigation
entry.content: Don, I restructured all in one single page just to closely resemble the KBA for another, as well as made as very simple bare bones as possible to make it simple. Still, as said before, the user input is taken programatically by the web form and then used to set the report parameter accordingly. I have gave i another try, this time with CrystalReportViewer1.EnableParameterPrompt = true as you said. The first time the report is run, say the inpout is 100, which is used to generate the dataset accoridngly, and to set the param to the report. The report renders fine , and you can see it used the 100 param. when clicking next page in the report, the "please wait while the document is being processed" spinning wheel appears forever. One has to close the browser. The report is actually very simple, but has a lot of rows (~77K). I am going to use NPOI instead since CR viewer is not working for me in this case. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: crystal viewer parameter loses value on navigation
entry.content: Here is the entire code, very much simplified as said before. using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Linq; using System.Web; using System.Globalization; using System.Data; using System.Data.SqlClient; using System.Reflection; using System.Text; using CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine; public partial class reports_T980010 : System.Web.UI.Page { String sYear = null; // Total count of qualifying students. // This is used to show in the execution report int mQualifyingStudentCount = 0; protected ReportDocument crReportDocument; protected List<ReportParam> reportParams; protected DataSet reportDataset; protected void Page_Init(object sender, EventArgs e) { Utils.doDebug("Page_Init() IsPostBack? " + IsPostBack.ToString()); if (IsPostBack) { crReportDocument = (ReportDocument)Session["Report"]; CrystalReportViewer1.ReportSource = crReportDocument; } } protected void btnReportSubmit_Click(object sender, EventArgs e) { String methodName = MethodBase.GetCurrentMethod().DeclaringType + "." + MethodBase.GetCurrentMethod().Name; int i= 1; try { bool isSessionNull = Session["Report"] == null; Utils.doDebug("btnReportSubmit_Click() isSessionNull? " + isSessionNull.ToString()); if (Session["Report"] == null) { crReportDocument = new ReportDocument(); // crviewer preferences CrystalReportViewer1.ToolPanelView = CrystalDecisions.Web.ToolPanelViewType.None; CrystalReportViewer1.HasToggleGroupTreeButton = false; CrystalReportViewer1.HasToggleParameterPanelButton = false; CrystalReportViewer1.HasPageNavigationButtons = true; CrystalReportViewer1.EnableDrillDown = false; // CrystalReportViewer1.ReuseParameterValuesOnRefresh = true; // 10/15/24 CrystalReportViewer1.EnableParameterPrompt = true; i = 2; // load report string reportViewUrl = @"~/reports/T980010Report.rpt"; String reportFullPathName = HttpContext.Current.Server.MapPath(reportViewUrl); crReportDocument.Load(reportFullPathName); i = 3; sYear = Utils.getNonNullString(txtYear.Text); // get user input reportDataset = generateReportDataSet(sYear); //generate corresponding dataset i = 4; // set report datasource crReportDocument.SetDataSource(reportDataset.Tables[0]); // set report params AFTER setting the report DataSource // to prevent the "missing parameter Value" error. reportParams = new List<ReportParam>(); string p_test = mQualifyingStudentCount.ToString(); // loses value i = 6; crReportDocument.SetParameterValue("p_test", p_test); i = 7; // Add report to the session Session.Add("Report", crReportDocument); i = 8; // set crviewer report source CrystalReportViewer1.ReportSource = crReportDocument; // show report } else { i = 10; crReportDocument = (ReportDocument)Session["Report"]; i = 20; CrystalReportViewer1.ReportSource = crReportDocument; // show report } } catch (Exception ex) { Utils.doDebug("Exception caught in " + methodName + ": " + ex.Message + " . line: " + i.ToString()); } } protected DataSet generateReportDataSet(string strYear) { String methodName = MethodBase.GetCurrentMethod().DeclaringType + "." + MethodBase.GetCurrentMethod().Name; DataSet ds = null; try { ds = buildDatasetStructure(); DataTable dt = ds.Tables[0]; populateData(strYear, ref dt); // transferFileS3(mOutputFileShortName); } catch (Exception ex) { Utils.doDebug("Exception caught in " + methodName + ": " + ex.Message); } return ds; } void populateData(string strYear, ref DataTable dt) { String methodName = MethodBase.GetCurrentMethod().DeclaringType + "." + MethodBase.GetCurrentMethod().Name; String errMessage = String.Empty; DataTable dtResults = null; try { dtResults = obtainUniversePopulationData(strYear); mQualifyingStudentCount = dtResults.Rows.Count; DataRow row = null; DataRow dr = null; String dataLine = String.Empty; for (int i = 0; i <= mQualifyingStudentCount - 1; i++) { // read the row row = dtResults.Rows[i]; dataLine = Utils.getNonNullString(row["STC_PERSON_ID"]); // create the row dr = dt.NewRow(); dr["STC_PERSON_ID"] = dataLine; // add row to the report data table dt.Rows.Add(dr); } } catch (Exception ex) { Utils.doDebug("Exception caught in " + methodName + ": " + ex.Message); } } protected void Page_UnLoad(object sender, EventArgs e) { try { crReportDocument.Close(); crReportDocument.Dispose(); } catch { } } private DataTable obtainUniversePopulationData(string p_year) { MethodBase method = MethodBase.GetCurrentMethod(); String methodName = method.DeclaringType + "." + method.Name; SqlConnection cn = null; DataSet ds = null; SqlCommand cmd = null; StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); DataTable dtResults = new DataTable(); try { cn = Lib.openDatatelConnection(false); sb.Append("SELECT TOP ").Append(p_year).Append(" ID AS STC_PERSON_ID FROM PERSON WITH (NOLOCK) "); string sql = sb.ToString(); cmd = Utils.getCommand(cn, sql); ds = Lib.getDataSet(cmd); dtResults = ds.Tables[0]; } catch (Exception ex){ Utils.doDebug("Exception caught in " + methodName + ": " + ex.Message); } finally { Utils.closeConnection(ref cn); } return dtResults; } private DataSet buildDatasetStructure() { DataTable dt = new DataTable(); dt.Columns.Add(new DataColumn("STC_PERSON_ID")); DataSet ds = new DataSet(); ds.Tables.Add(dt); return ds; } }

entry.title: Re: Usage and differences between MRS , RSH and FSM
entry.content:
Hello Ryan,

  I am analyzing these products mentioned in the post question for implementation in my organization.
  As far as I could see the SAP MRS 10.0 has as successor according to the PAM the “SAP Multiresource Scheduling, optimizer version 1.0 for SAP S/4HANA (SAP MRS W/OPT FOR S4HANA 1.0)” and this in turn as successor in the PAM does not yield any product, although its maintenance is until 31.12.2030.
  The question is, if I have in my ECC 6.0 implemented SAP MRS 9.0 or 10.0 and I have to make the system conversion to S/4HANA 2023 (for example) to continue with the SAP roadmap in the ERP strategy, to which product is converted the MRS 9.0 or 10.0? Thanks in advance,

Kind regards!

Sebastián.

entry.title: What's the purpose of the Caching section in the 'Edit Endpoint' menu of the Commerce Cloud portal?
entry.content:
What is the purpose of the Caching section within the 'Edit Endpoint' menu of the SAP Commerce Cloud portal?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: What's the purpose of the Caching section in the 'Edi...
entry.content:
The purpose of the Caching section within the 'Edit Endpoint' menu of the SAP Commerce Cloud portal is to enhance the performance and efficiency of your storefront by enabling effective management of web caching. Web caching can significantly improve the speed of web page loading and reduce the load on commerce pods, particularly for static files.

Benefits for Non-Production Environments: For non-production loads, the integrated caching solution can be configured when creating or editing an endpoint using the Cloud Portal. For more information, see Creating an Endpoint.

For production environments, however, it is recommended to use a Content Delivery Network (CDN) for optimized performance, as CDNs are designed to handle large-scale traffic and provide global content distribution.

How Web Caching Works: The caching mechanism within the SAP Commerce Cloud portal involves the Apache protocol that runs within the endpoint. This protocol keeps the cached data and serves it to unique client browsers until the cached data expires. This minimizes the need for repetitive data fetching from the server, thus speeding up the response time.

Considerations for Effective Caching:
  1. Static File Caching:
    • Only static files can be effectively cached. Examples include images, CSS files, and JavaScript files that do not frequently change. This ensures a stable and high-performance user experience.
  2. Avoiding Dynamic Content Caching:
    • If you cache objects that change often, such as API endpoints, scripts, or dynamically generated content, the caching feature may not work as intended. This can lead to outdated content being served, which impacts the site's effectiveness.
  3. Compliance with CDN:
    • When using an external CDN solution alongside web caching, ensure that expiry times are aligned. Misaligned expiry times can lead to performance issues because the CDN already takes care of caching. It's crucial to analyze and conduct performance tests before activating web caching to avoid potential conflicts and inefficiencies.
  4. VARY Header Utilization:
    • If your application must respond with different static content based on request headers (using the VARY header), remember that this will result in multiple copies of the same URL with different content being cached. This can take up considerable cache space and should be managed carefully.
  5. Performance Monitoring:
    • If you experience site latency, use the web browser’s developer tools to analyze network metrics, noting the number of requests and time spent on each. Logs from OpenSearch can also provide insights into resource-loading errors.
  6. Static Content and Customizations:
    • Check if any resources are taking too long to load and review storefront customizations that may affect these resources. Latency issues with JavaScript or CSS files can often be addressed by optimizing the web cache settings.
Technical Specifications:
  • The web caching feature stores files on an Azure Premium SSD disk, with a limit of 120 IOPS and a total cached data limit of 5 GiB.
  • The cumulative length of all path URLs in web caching must be below 1024 characters to ensure smooth operation.

For a comprehensive guide and more details, refer to the official documentation here: Web Caching.

By carefully managing these aspects, the Caching section in the SAP Commerce Cloud portal can be a powerful tool for improving storefront performance and ensuring a responsive, user-friendly experience.

entry.title: ABAP RAP Unmanaged - Created record not showing on Object Page
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I have created V4 UI app using Unmanaged RAP Scenario... When I am trying to create record using Preview option, records are getting created but they are not showing up on Object Page immediately after creation. I have Implemented Early Numbering which is getting triggered along with Create Method...

This works in ABAP cloud Trial system...

I am on S4HANA ON PREMISE.

Please help what am I missing...

Early Numbering code

 

 

METHOD earlynumbering_create.

    DATA: l_tanum         TYPE /scwm/tanum,
          l_returncode(1) TYPE c.
    CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
      EXPORTING
        nr_range_nr        = '01'
        object             = wmegc_nrobj_to
        subobject          = 'WHNO'
      IMPORTING
        number             = l_tanum
        returncode         = l_returncode
      EXCEPTIONS
        interval_not_found = 1.
    LOOP AT entities ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_entities>).
      l_tanum = l_tanum + 1.
      mapped-warehousetask  =   VALUE #(
        FOR ls_entities IN entities WHERE ( tanum is INITIAL )
        (
            %cid    = ls_entities-%cid
            %is_draft   =   ls_entities-%is_draft
            tanum   =   l_tanum
        )
      ).
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

Behavior Definition

 

 

unmanaged implementation in class zbp_i_openwt unique;
strict;
with draft;
define behavior for ZI_OPENWT alias WarehouseTask
draft table Zordim_o_d
//late numbering
early numbering
lock master
total etag ChangeAt
authorization master ( global )
etag master ChangeAt
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly ) Tanum;
  field ( readonly ) CreatedBy;
  field ( readonly ) Letyp;
  field ( readonly ) CreatedAt;
  draft action Edit;
  draft action Activate;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare;
  mapping for /scwm/ordim_o {
    Lgnum   =   lgnum;
    Tanum   =   tanum;
    Letyp   = letyp;
    Procty  = procty;
    Tostat  =   tostat;
    Vlenr   =   vlenr;
    ChangeAt    =   changed_at;
    CreatedAt   =   created_at;
    CreatedBy   =   created_by;
  }
}

 

 

CREATE method

 

 

  METHOD create.
    DATA: lo_entity_store TYPE REF TO zcl_datastore_rap,
          ls_entity       TYPE /scwm/s_ordim_o_rf.
    lo_entity_store = zcl_datastore_rap=>get_object(  ).
    LOOP AT entities ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_owt>).
      ls_entity-vlenr_o   = <lfs_owt>-vlenr.
      ls_entity-nlpla_o   = <lfs_owt>-nlpla.
      INSERT VALUE #( %cid = <lfs_owt>-%cid ) INTO TABLE mapped-warehousetask.
    ENDLOOP.
    lo_entity_store->put_data( data_in = ls_entity ).
  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

Save method

 

 

METHOD save.
    DATA: lt_ltap_vb      TYPE    /scwm/tt_ltap_vb,
          lt_bapiret      TYPE    bapiret2_t,
          lo_entity_store TYPE REF TO zcl_datastore_rap,
          ls_entity       TYPE /scwm/s_ordim_o_rf,
          lv_severity     TYPE    bapi_mtype.
    lo_entity_store = zcl_datastore_rap=>get_object(  ).
    ls_entity = lo_entity_store->get_data(  ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'ZEWM_PWT_CREATE'
      EXPORTING
        iv_lgnum    = 'WHNO'
        iv_huident  = ls_entity-vlenr_o
        iv_nlpla    = ls_entity-nlpla_o
      IMPORTING
        et_ltap_vb  = lt_ltap_vb
        et_bapiret  = lt_bapiret
        ev_severity = lv_severity.

    IF lv_severity CA 'ISW'.
      LOOP AT lt_bapiret INTO DATA(ls_bapiret) WHERE type = 'S'.
        APPEND VALUE #( %msg = new_message_with_text(
            text = ls_bapiret-message severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-success
        )
        %key-tanum = lt_ltap_vb[ 1 ]-tanum
        tanum = lt_ltap_vb[ 1 ]-tanum
         ) TO reported-warehousetask.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

Please help.

@junwu @Marian_Zeis 

Thanks-

entry.title: Re: BusinessObjects - Trending DB Configuration for Oracle
entry.content:
Bernhard, thanks for the information. What you mentioned is basically what I was following but had more to do.

I found the following SAP Note to be very helpful for finding what else I needed to do.  Read the steps.  Do not view the video.

  • 1768678 - HOWTO - Configure Oracle Audit database for Monitoring [Video] BI 4.X - SAP for Me

In reagrds to Oracle 19c usage, I have the Oracle.sbo file confugred in the Oracle 19c section and it appears to be working.  As I mentioned before, this is BOB43, SP4, Patch 7.

There was another tricky step that needed to be done which is mentioned in the SAP Note:  WE ARE RUNNING ON WINDOWS SERVER

  • Remove the double forward slash and change the single forward slash to colon in the following line: <Parameter Name="URL Format">jdbc:oracle:thin:@$DATASOURCE$:$DATABASE$</Parameter>

Of course the first step was to run the SQL statements in the Audit Data Store Oracle database to create the necessary database tables.

If interested, here are the steps I documented for myself:

Get SQL commands from the oracle.sql file located in the following directory:
	D:\SAP BusinessObjects\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\Data\TrendingDB
Logon to the Oracle database for the Audit Data Store.
Run the SQL commands.
Logoff from the Oracle database.
Make a backup copy of the oracle.sbo file located in the following directory:
	D:\SAP BusinessObjects\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\dataAccess\connectionServer\jdbc
Edit the oracle.sbo file.
Find the following <Database...> tag for Oracle 19c:
	<DataBase Active="Yes" Name="Oracle 19c">
Add the folowing lines immediately following the <Database...> tag:
	<Aliases>
		<Alias>Oracle</Alias>
	</Aliases>
Add the following lines immediately following the <JDBCDriver> tag:
	<ClassPath>
<Path>D:\Oracle19c64\product\19.0.0\client_1\jdbc\lib\ojdbc8.jar</Path>
	</ClassPath>
Remove the double forward slash and change the single forward slash to colon in the following line:
	<Parameter Name="URL Format">jdbc:oracle:thin:@$DATASOURCE$:$DATABASE$</Parameter>
Final result of changes should look like the following:
	<DataBase Active="Yes" Name="Oracle 19c">
		<Aliases>
			<Alias>Oracle</Alias>
		</Aliases>
		<JDBCDriver>
			<ClassPath>
<Path>D:\Oracle19c64\product\19.0.0\client_1\jdbc\lib\ojdbc8.jar</Path>
			</ClassPath>	
			<Parameter Name="JDBC Class">oracle.jdbc.OracleDriver</Parameter>
			<Parameter Name="URL Format">jdbc:oracle:thin:@$DATASOURCE$:$DATABASE$</Parameter>
		</JDBCDriver>
		. . .
Save and close the oracle.sbo file.
Logon to the Central Management Console.
Change the ADS Database Connection Name to the following:
	<server/host name>,<port number>,<SID>
Logoff from the Central Management Console.
Edit the tnsnames.ora file located in the following directory:
		D:\Oracle19c64\product\19.0.0\client_1\network\admin
Find the entry for the database used for the Audit Data Store.
Duplicate the entry for the database used for the Audit Data Store.
Remove the ".company.com" from the alias name in the duplicate entry. [see step 6 in the SAP Note]
There should now be two entries for the same database, using short [SID] and fully qualified alias names [SID.company.com].  We use fully qualified alias names for Connections for Universes so I need to keep them along with the short/just SID entry.
	[SID].company.com	= ...
	[SID]				= ...
Save and close the tnsnames.ora file.
Stop and Restart the SIA.
Logon to the Central Management Console.
Access the properties for the BI Admin Studio application (Monitoring).
Change the Trending Database Settings to use the Audit Database.
Save and Close the setting changes.
Restart the APSOtherServices server.

entry.title: Moving SAP Screen Personas flavors from ECC to SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
We have been receiving some inquiries from customers who want to move their SAP Screen Personas flavors from ECC to SAP S/4HANA. I got the sense from the request that they were looking for some kind of wizard that would review the flavors, determine what field IDs changed, and update all the scripts accordingly. Since that does not exist, I’ll share some guidance on a process that will help you with your UX transformation as part of an SAP S/4HANA migration project. This applies when moving to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP S/4HANA (on-prem).

While an automated solution would be great, let’s consider the challenges of creating such a tool. There are thousands of classic apps in SAP S/4HANA. There are potentially even more ways that a customer might customize those apps, including creating custom fields, merging fields from different tabs within the same app, or integrating data across multiple transactions / apps.

As many technologists in Silicon Valley and around the world know, the perfect is the enemy of the good. So, rather than aiming for a 1-click fix that addresses every situation, we’ll discuss some ways for you to approach the process while, in parallel, we explore ways to use our inside knowledge of the transaction structure and field IDs to help find areas that might cause trouble with flavor migrations.

Let me start by stating the obvious. SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA are very different products. Yes, they are both ERP systems, just as the Ford Model T and Ford Mustang Mach E are both vehicles that will help you address your transportation needs. SAP S/4HANA was engineered to leverage the strengths of an in-memory database to provide better and faster insights based on the real-time needs of modern enterprises.

SAP S/4HANA uses SAP Fiori as the main user experience

Another big difference is that SAP S/4HANA is designed to use SAP Fiori as the main user experience. Classic apps still exist in SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud and the option to use SAP Screen Personas or Adapt UI for Classic Apps remains if you want to simplify them to make them look and behave more like SAP Fiori apps. While some apps in SAP S/4HANA are like their predecessors in ECC, there are some factors to consider when thinking about moving your flavors during your upgrade.

1. Evaluate changes to process flows between ECC and SAP S/4HANA

There is generally not a 1:1 relationship between SAP Fiori apps and ECC transaction codes. In some cases, a single transaction will become several SAP Fiori apps as the model for SAP Fiori is to keep apps simple, focused, and role based. In other cases, several transactions might be consolidated into a single SAP Fiori app, if the app addresses a single business process for a specific role.

So, the first step before thinking about moving a flavor from ECC to SAP S/4HANA is to check whether an SAP Fiori app exists to accomplish the same task. If an SAP Fiori app exists, that will likely deliver a great out-of-the box experience with nothing custom to maintain. You can always adapt the app, if necessary.

2. Understand that classic applications evolve

If there’s no SAP Fiori app that does what you need, explore whether the corresponding Classic App in SAP S/4HANA introduced new functionality. Even if the assigned transaction code stays the same, the classic application could significantly differ between ECC and S/4HANA. For classic apps in SAP S/4HANA that add new capabilities, we recommend examining if the old flavor will leverage these new capabilities or if it is better to build a new flavor that exercises all the new stuff. If the new app and old transaction really are similar, we recommend thoroughly testing the flavor to ensure compatibility. 

3. Update themes to ensure visual harmonization in SAP S/4HANA

Flavors created for ECC systems likely use old SAP themes that generally require additional work to ensure that flavors look and perform with the latest SAP Fiori themes. This includes both functional and non-functional changes. For example, the next step in an SAP Fiori app is often accessed by clicking a button in the lower right corner. Classic apps have the next step in various places, so you would want to move “next”, “save”, or whatever to the lower right corner for visual consistency.
Another main difference in SAP Fiori themes is the header. Menu bar and toolbars are reorganized compared to old themes.  

4. Ensure that flavors work in My Home as a single-entry point

My Home is the starting point for SAP S/4HANA. You access your apps from there, whether they are assigned to you, you favorite them, or you accept the system recommendations based on your business role. My Home replaces the SAP Fiori launchpad, which looks rather drab in comparison.

Typically, flavors in ECC are accessed using the direct service URL. Using flavors in My Home is possible but requires some adoption to ensure flavors are correctly assigned to business roles and function correctly in the SAP Fiori launchpad service that launches flavors from My Home. 

5. Look at the best UI technology to meet your needs – they are always evolving

There are many technologies that might be part of your SAP Screen Personas deployment on ECC. Consider which ones make sense to use in the SAP S/4HANA context.

  • SAP GUI for HTML – this is the standard rendering engine for classic apps. If your flavors are optimized to run on SAP GUI for HTML, you are in good shape to migrate them.
  • SAP GUI for Windows - We don’t recommend using it with SAP Screen Personas due to lack of development and switch to Web technologies. Migrate your flavors to WebGUI if you want to use them in SAP S/4HANA.
  • Slipstream Engine – this was our recommended approach for building flavors for mobile use-cases. It is supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA on-prem, but not SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The built-in SAP Screen Personas in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that is accessed through the Adapt UI menu only runs on SAP GUI for HTML.
  • MOBGUI – for EWM_RFUI ITS Mobile replacement. This service has no title bar, toolbars, or menu bar, so if child controls are used in a flavor, they will not be available and a script button alternative must be used. For best results, we recommend that you use the latest versions of SAP Screen Personas and SAP S/4HANA (SP18 and 2023 FPS2, respectively, at the time of writing).
  • Web Dynpro ABAP – We recommend using options other than SAP Screen Personas to customize them, as explained in note 3239603.

6. Retire heavy custom development to follow clean core principles

Some customers use SAP Screen Personas to implement entirely new functionality. They create new screens that merge content from multiple transactions and then use complex scripts (1000+ lines in some cases) to perform updates to multiple transactions. Migrating such custom development from ECC to S/4HANA is not supported by SAP and would require extensive testing and maintenance effort on the customer side. If you want to create new functionality, we recommend building a custom app using SAP Fiori elements. The combination of SAP Build Code, SAP Fiori elements, SAP Fiori tools, and SAP Fiori AI simplifies the process and will provide a better result with significantly lower maintenance costs.

7. Reuse Control and Screen overwrite rules

If custom Control ID and Screen ID overwrite rules are used on ECC, they must be recreated on S/4HANA. Otherwise, Flavor changes will not be applied.

Summary (TL;DR)

  1. Evaluate changes to process flows between ECC and SAP S/4HANA
  2. Understand that classic applications evolve
  3. Update themes to ensure visual harmonization in SAP S/4HANA
  4. Ensure that flavors work in My Home as a single-entry point
  5. Look at the best UI technology to meet your needs – they are always evolving
  6. Retire heavy custom development to follow clean core principles
  7. Reuse Control and Screen overwrite rules

 

Good luck with your UX transformation projects. We have many resources to help you on your journey:

  • Take the SAP Learning course on using SAP Screen Personas with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Follow the SAP Learning course on developing apps with SAP Fiori elements
  • Subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter

Thanks to Krists Magons, Tamas Hoznek, and Vladimirs Ananko for their contributions to this post.

entry.title: Using 'State' on Location to drive logic for State Notices on 'Home Page Cards' in Onboarding.
entry.content:
In the USA each state has a number of ‘State Notices’ and based on the location of their employment you may need to display the correct ‘State Notices’ during the onboarding process.  In this article we will look at a way you use homepage cards and rules to display the correct notices depending on the new hires work location.

In SuccessFactors Employee Central each location has an address associated.  However, you can’t use the ‘State’ of the ‘corporate address’ to trigger the logic as this is an association hence you can’t reference it in the rules engine.

1. Create a custom field called ‘USA State’ on the location object and attach the standard ‘USA_state’ picklist, this field is available in the rules engine.

2. Create rules using the Scenario ‘Show New Hire Onboarding Content’ as shown below.  In theory you will need to create one for each state where you have employees, but you can utilize the ‘copy’ feature to speed up the rule creation.

3. Create a new home page card and assign this to all external users in USA,  This has been set up to show the card 60 days before and 30 days after their hire date, but this can be adjusted accordingly.

4. The card should have ‘rule based settings’ and against each rule we assign specific text and links

For the onboardee it appears like this based on their state of the location of their position/jobinfo record.

You can use the same logic on the location object to custom data collection and assigning forms. An example is show below.

entry.title: Re: "undefinedprocess completed successfully" Message at...
entry.content:
Hi,

It’s very hard to tell anything without checking your setup and doing some debugging.
Does your PPD use “POD Notification” service? This toast message seems to be sent by it.

Some things you can check:

  • Check browser Developer Console, Console and Network tabs, and try to understand what is happening and which notifications are coming and what is being sent / received.
  • Perhaps there is an Automatic Trigger being fired by your PPD call or by the Operation Complete  itself, triggering another PPD that is sending this POD Notification back? Check Monitor Production Processes app to see which PPDs are being triggered.

Otherwise, I highly suggest you raise a ticket for proper investigation and support.

entry.title: Re: Access Policy for securing design artifacts in the SAP Cloud Integration designer
entry.content:
Hi Kamlesh,

I'm currently working with both Access Policy types. However, I've encountered an issue related to how access is managed for our packages. We have two categories of packages: those intended solely for internal use (Internal_Package_01, Internal_Package_02) and those designed for external use (External_Package_01, External_Package_02). My goal is for internal users to access both internal and external packages while restricting external users to only the external packages.

My current solution involves creating Package-level Access Policies for the external packages and Artifact-level Access Policies for all artifacts, assigning these policies accordingly. However, this approach has led to a complication: internal users, upon accessing an external package, cannot save any artifacts. They encounter an error message stating, "You are not authorized to save the package due to package-level access policies. Contact your tenant administrator for access."

Could you suggest alternative strategies or improvements to address this situation more effectively?

Thank you

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: can not have product recommendation in email campaign
entry.content:
For Recently Viewed recommendation logic, the system will only show exact items last viewed by the visitor and will use Mail Personal based on recent browsing activity and purchase history.

You can click here to check the Emarsys help page for more details.
 

Therefore if the contact does not have any browsing history registered, the recommendation logic is not able to provide any recommendations.

In order to use Recently Viewed is necessary to have product view history for the widget to work accordingly. 

Please notice that the recommendation Recently Viewed take into consideration only those individual views and purchases that happened in the last 30 days. Contacts who haven't viewed or purchased a product for 30 days may receive a recommendation of a product they have never browsed or purchased.

entry.title: Re: Estimating Mean and Variance Through Backpropagation
entry.content:
Errata:

The line:

The solution is to have your model output two heads: one for the mean of the value you are predicting and one for the variance of .

should read:

The solution is to have your model output two heads: one for the mean of y and one for the variance of y. 
 
The line:
 
But on the other hand, we directly minimize the log(sigma) term. Since log(sigma) is proportional to just sigma, that means we want to minimize the variance. That also makes sense... we can't let backprop greedily just minimize the loss term by estimating an infinite variance.

should read:

But on the other hand, we directly minimize the log(sigma) term. Since log(x) and x and monotonic transformations (the signs of the gradients of the two functions are the same), that means we are minimizing the variance / stdev. That also makes sense... we can't let backprop greedily just minimize the loss term by estimating an infinite variance / stdev.

 

The stack of dense layers has a bug. It should read as below. The results remain reproducible. 

 

        x = x + self.act2(self.dense2(x))
        x = x + self.act3(self.dense3(x))
        x = x + self.act4(self.dense4(x))
        x = x + self.act5(self.dense5(x))
        x = x + self.act6(self.dense6(x))
        x = x + self.act7(self.dense7(x))
        x = x + self.act8(self.dense8(x))
        x = x + self.act9(self.dense9(x))

entry.title: SAP Transaction T-Codes History Not Showing / Not Saving Error
entry.content:
Hello SAP guys,

When you're updating the MS Office package on your PC pay attention as it may sometimes cause the SAP GUI history to not display.

The main cause of this is that SAP's main data is stored on the PC using MS Access. When you update MS Office it may not function well with the older stored files and new data not saved.

To fix this issue:

  1. Close all SAP sessions first.
  2. Go to Windows All Programs > Search for SAP GUI Configuration.
  3.  In GUI Configuration > Local Data > History.
  4. 4.Make sure the History Status is set to "On".
  5. Then navigate to: C:\Users\<User Name>\AppData\Roaming\SAP\SAP GUI\History.
  6. If you can’t find the file, copy the full file location. Click on the My Computer icon, paste the copied file path into the search bar and the file will open automatically.
  7. You should now see three files there.
  8. Right-click on the .mdb file and rename it to SAPHistory<SAP Username>.mdb.
  9. Restart your PC and check. You should now see all T-codes being saved.

 

I believe this will help you fix this issue.

Thank You.
